meta.title: Expiatorio Park | Guadalajara
meta.description: Explore Expiatorio Park in Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico. Discover this beautiful urban park.

intro.title: Explore Expiatorio Park
intro.description: Expiatorio Park is located in the Col Americana neighborhood of Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico. It is a beautiful urban park adjacent to the magnificent Templo Expiatorio, offering visitors a great place to relax and enjoy culture and gastronomy.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: The park is open all day, daytime visits are recommended
Enjoy the green spaces and walking paths
Great for family outings and recreational activities
Explore the nearby Col Americana neighborhood

alsoKnownAs.title: Highlights
items: Urban Park: Located in Col Americana, Guadalajara, with beautiful surroundings.
Recreational Space: Offers green spaces and walking paths for relaxation and exercise.
Family-Friendly: Suitable for family outings with children's play facilities.
Prime Location: Located in Guadalajara, Jalisco, easily accessible.

route.title: Tour Route
route.overview: Discover the ideal route to explore Plaza de los Mariachis and experience the essence of Mexican music and culture.
steps: Arrive at the plaza in the afternoon to admire the surrounding colonial architecture and historical plaques.
In the evening, find a seat at an outdoor restaurant or bar to enjoy traditional tequila and Mexican cuisine.
As night falls, watch live performances by Mariachi bands and feel the lively musical atmosphere.
After your visit, walk to the nearby San Juan de Dios Market to shop for souvenirs.
supplements: Please prepare some small denomination peso cash for tipping the performing musicians.
The area around the plaza can be crowded, so please take care of your personal belongings.
If you wish to request a song, you can prepare the names of your favorite traditional Mexican songs in advance.

knowledge.title: Discover the Charm of Plaza de los Mariachis

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.content: Plaza de los Mariachis is the center of Guadalajara's cultural and musical tradition. Mariachi music originated in 19th century Jalisco and has been recognized by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. The plaza is where musicians gather to perform.

sections.title: Cultural Landscape
sections.content: The plaza is located in Guadalajara's historic center, surrounded by colonial-style buildings. It's an excellent place to experience traditional Mexican culture and music, full of vitality and color.

sections.title: Visiting Experience
sections.content: Visiting Plaza de los Mariachis is an unforgettable cultural experience. You can listen to live Mariachi music performances, taste traditional Mexican food, and feel the passion and vitality of Mexican culture.

basicInfo.title: Basic Information
basicInfo.addressValue: Col Americana, Americana, 44160 Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico

hours.title: Opening Hours
hours.summerTime: Pleasant weather, ideal for outdoor visits
hours.winterTime: Warm weather with afternoon rains, bring umbrella
hours.warningTime: Recommended to verify latest information before visiting
hours.tip: Evening visits offer the best music performances

tickets.title: Ticket Information
tickets.studentsPrice: Many restaurants and food stalls nearby

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: Nearest airport: Guadalajara International Airport (GDL). From there, take a bus or taxi to the historic center.
transport.selfDriveDesc: The plaza is located in the historic center. Use navigation and check for public parking nearby.
transport.busDesc: Take Guadalajara metro or bus to Historic Center station, then walk to the plaza.
transport.intercityDesc: The plaza is in the historic center, walkable to nearby attractions.
transport.cyclingDesc: Public transport or walking recommended, historic center traffic can be congested.
transport.tipsDesc: Plaza de los Mariachis is an excellent place to experience Mexican music and culture, evening visits recommended.

items.name: Juan Carlos
items.text: A magical place full of music and culture. The live Mariachi performances are unforgettable, highly recommended for all tourists.

items.name: María Elena
items.text: Amazing cultural experience. The musicians are very professional and the music is full of passion. This is the essence of Mexican culture.

items.name: Roberto Méndez
items.text: Very interesting place to experience authentic Mariachi music. Evening visits recommended when musicians start performing.

items.name: Ana Lucía
items.text: An important part of Mexican culture. The musicians' performances are moving, a must-visit place in Guadalajara.

items.name: Carlos Herrera
items.text: Great cultural experience. The music performances are wonderful, and there are many food stalls nearby.

items.name: Sofía Vargas
items.text: Beautiful plaza, full of the charm of Mexican culture. The musicians' performances are unforgettable, definitely worth a visit.

items.name: Miguel Ángel
items.text: Interesting cultural venue. Experience Mariachi music up close, tips appreciated to show respect to musicians.

items.name: Laura Patricia
items.text: Cultural gem of Guadalajara. Mariachi music is UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, definitely worth experiencing.

officialManagement.title: About Plaza de los Mariachis
officialManagement.text: Plaza de los Mariachis is a cultural landmark in Guadalajara, and Mariachi music is recognized by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage.

aboutExpiatorio.title: Templo Expiatorio: An Icon of Guadalajara

sections.title: History of the Expiatorio
sections.description: The history of the Templo Expiatorio dates back to the 19th century. Under the direction of Archbishop Pedro Loza y Pardavé, the project by prominent Italian architect Adamo Boari was selected. Construction began in 1897 and was not completed until 1972, taking 75 years due to the Mexican Revolution and economic crises.

sections.title: A Neo-Gothic Masterpiece
sections.description: The temple is a magnificent example of Neo-Gothic style, built mainly with carved stone. A particularly striking feature is its German-made clock with a musical carillon; every day at 9 a.m., 12 p.m., and 6 p.m., figures of the twelve apostles emerge. It is considered a masterpiece of Neo-Gothic architecture in Mexico.

sections.title: A Religious and Gastronomic Gathering
sections.description: The Expiatorio offers more than a spiritual experience; its esplanade is a vibrant place full of entertainment and delicious food options. You can enjoy pastries, elotes, tacos, and more. A few steps away, you can also explore the Museum of the Arts (MUSA).

sections.title: Why is it called Expiatorio?
sections.description: Although its official name is the Basilica of the Blessed Sacrament, it was given the name 'Expiatorio' in 1906 for ceremonies of expiation. It is said that if you visit the Pearl of the West and do not stop by the Expiatorio, it is as if you were never here.
